articles.title: Standard Chartered Bank settles Sebi case over FPI lapses
articles.description: Standard Chartered Bank has settled a case with market regulator Sebi. The bank agreed to pay Rs 57 lakh for alleged lapses as a designated depository participant for foreign portfolio investors. Sebi found failures in monitoring FPI disclosures and reporting changes in beneficial ownership. Delays in processing investor grouping updates were also noted. The settlement resolves multiple compliance issues.

articles.title: CFM ARC wins bid for PNB's Gammon debt at 73% haircut
articles.description: CFM Asset Reconstruction Company has acquired Punjab National Bank's ₹514 crore loan to Gammon India for ₹140 crore. This acquisition gives CFM control over a significant portion of Gammon's legacy non-performing asset, primarily targeting its real estate assets. The deal represents a substantial haircut for PNB, highlighting the challenges in recovering debt from EPC companies.

articles.title: Standard Chartered Bank settles Sebi case over FPI lapses
articles.description: Standard Chartered Bank has settled a case with market regulator Sebi. The bank agreed to pay Rs 57 lakh for alleged lapses as a designated depository participant for foreign portfolio investors. Sebi found failures in monitoring FPI disclosures and reporting changes in beneficial ownership. Delays in processing investor grouping updates were also noted. The settlement resolves multiple compliance issues.

articles.description: Pagdi specialist recalls his work on ‘Dhurandhar’ set, Ranveer Singh’s praise: ‘’Kya mujhe bhi classes mil sakti hain?’  The Indian Express

articles.title: Stock Market Today: All You Need To Know Before Going Into Trade On Wednesday
articles.description: Stocks in the news, big brokerage calls of the day, complete trade setup and much more!

topStories.title: Top court pushes govt for paternity leave framework
topStories.description: The Supreme Court urged the government to establish a paternity leave framework, emphasizing its role in promoting gender equality and child welfare.

topStories.title: SC orders maternity leave for all adoptive mothers -- irrespective of age of child
topStories.description: The Supreme Court ruled that all adoptive mothers are entitled to 12 weeks of maternity leave, regardless of the child's age, enhancing parental rights.

topStories.title: Dubai, Abu Dhabi, UAE News LIVE Updates: Blasts rock Dubai, US deploys bunker-buster bombs near Strait of Hormuz
topStories.description: Dubai, Abu Dhabi, UAE News LIVE: According to officials, the U.S.-Israeli war has resulted in at least 1,300 deaths in Iran, over 900 in Lebanon, and 12 in Israel.

topStories.title: Total repression and air strikes bring unrelenting dread for Iranians
topStories.description: Tehran residents tell the BBC they're caught between US-Israeli bombing and an Iranian regime trying to reassert its power.

topStories.title: Israel killed Larijani hoping to ‘torpedo’ chance of US-Iran talks
topStories.description: Marwan Bishara says Israel’s assassination of Larijani is calculated to prolong the war and bring about regime change.

topStories.title: Success or defeat of no-confidence motion is irrelevant
topStories.description: Congress lawmaker and senior advocate Abhishek Singhvi in an interview with HT speaks on cross-voting by members of his party in the just-concluded Rajya Sabha polls as well as multiple no-confidence notices the Opposition has brought against constitutional functionaries

topStories.title: Chile’s President Kast tosses out dozens of environmental protections
topStories.description: Chilean Environment Ministry suspends 43 measures touching on issues such as pollution, emissions and national parks.

topStories.title: "Don't Need Any Help": Trump Fumes At NATO Over Hormuz Support Refusal
topStories.description: Trump fumed that the US is not getting support "despite the fact that we helped" NATO "so much," and said that it was in allies' interest to prevent Iran from securing a nuclear weapon.

topStories.title: Ireland’s Micheal Martin and Trump in awkward St Patrick’s Day exchange
topStories.description: Ireland, which took strong stance on Gaza genocide, is similarly robust on Iran, but Martin is criticised for passivity.

topStories.title: Argentina officially withdraws from World Health Organization, following US
topStories.description: Argentinian President Javier Milei has criticised the global health body for its response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

topStories.title: US Carrier Involved in Iran Fight Heads Back to Port After Fire
topStories.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier is leaving the fight with Iran and heading back to port, a US official familiar with the matter said, after a fire broke out in its laundry area and left at least two sailors with non-life-threatening injuries.

topStories.title: Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war, urging Trump to 'reverse course'
topStories.description: National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent says Trump "started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby".

dailyNews.description: The attacks are part of an escalating cycle of violence between US forces and Iraqi groups aligned with Tehran.

dailyNews.description: Mr. Modi said he and the UAE President agreed on the importance of ensuring safe and free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz

dailyNews.title: Trump calls mail in voting corrupt as Senate begins debate on SAVE Act requiring voter ID
dailyNews.description: Trump calls mail-in voting 'corrupt as hell' as Senate advances SAVE America Act requiring voter ID and citizenship proof for federal elections nationwide.

dailyNews.title: Dems unmoved as White House reveals DHS concessions in shutdown battle
dailyNews.description: The White House revealed five immigration enforcement compromises to Dems as the government shutdown continues, including body cameras for DHS agents and beefed-up oversight.

dailyNews.title: White House, after top counterterrorism official quits, says Trump had 'strong' evidence Iran would attack US
dailyNews.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Tuesday that top counterterrorism official Joe Kent's resignation letter had "many false claims."

dailyNews.title: Year After Trump Takeover, Institute of Peace Is Little More Than Scenery
dailyNews.description: As a lawsuit by the former staff drags on, the fight highlights President Trump’s upending of traditional peace-building and the lasting effects of his administration’s cost-cutting blitz.

dailyNews.title: No Trump Endorsement for Cornyn or Paxton in Texas Senate Race as Deadline Passes
dailyNews.description: President Trump’s decision not to weigh in before the deadline means both John Cornyn and Ken Paxton remain on the ballot, extending their costly and increasingly personal battle into a May runoff.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Newsom Shouldn’t Be President Because He Is Dyslexic
dailyNews.description: President Trump said “a president should not have learning disabilities,” prompting criticism from a group that advocates equal opportunities for people with learning disabilities.

dailyNews.title: Why Did Trump Officials Award $2 Million to a Small Art School in Queens?
dailyNews.description: The National Endowment for the Humanities seldom gave seven-figure grants. Now big awards flow to handpicked projects, including an institution with three full-time employees.

dailyNews.title: Cuba plans to open up to investment from nationals in U.S. amid pressure from Trump
dailyNews.description: The Cuban government is planning to allow Cuban nationals who live abroad to invest in the island, a government official told NBC News, as the country faces economic collapse and pressure from the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Cuba faces deepening crisis as Trump mulls takeover
dailyNews.description: Cuba has been facing more blackouts and protests since the U.S. cut off its access to Venezuelan oil. Juan Palop reports from Havana.

dailyNews.title: Israel targets more Iran leaders as allies reject Trump's calls for help
dailyNews.description: Israel says it killed Iran's top security official Ali Larijani, as America's European allies reject President Trump's demands for help in the Strait of Hormuz.
